title: Melty British Cheese Yorkshire Pudding
description: A comforting, citrusy British dish featuring a cheese-pull Yorkshire pudding, perfect for a sick day. This recipe incorporates dill and a mantecare emulsification method.
instructions: Preheat the tava griddle over medium heat to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, eggs, milk, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Add the grated cheese, chopped dill, melted butter, and lemon zest to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Pour the batter onto the preheated tava griddle and cook for 5 minutes.
Reduce the heat to low and continue cooking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the pudding is golden brown and set.
Remove the pudding from the heat and let it cool for 2 minutes before slicing and serving.
To emulsify the cheese, use a spatula to gently fold the melted cheese into the pudding as it cools.
Serve the melty British cheese Yorkshire pudding hot, garnished with additional chopped dill if desired.
